danger_signals.visual_form: Rides deteriorate visually over time if unmaintained; eventual explosion animation
danger_signals.meaning: Neglected maintenance leads to catastrophic failure and visitor injury/death

danger_signals.visual_form: Sad visitor sprites/animations; reduced visitor count at entry
danger_signals.meaning: Park conditions have deteriorated; visitors will leave and thugs will arrive

danger_signals.visual_form: Thug character sprites appearing in park pathways
danger_signals.meaning: Crime is occurring; vandalism, theft, and violence against staff/entertainers ongoing

danger_signals.visual_form: Litter and debris accumulation on walkways
danger_signals.meaning: Staff shortage or inefficiency; poor park condition attracting negative behavior

danger_signals.visual_form: Staff absent from posts; shipment delivery icons missing or grayed
danger_signals.meaning: Negotiation failure; loss of workers or supply chain disruption

danger_signals.visual_form: Ride stops mid-operation; mechanical animation halts
danger_signals.meaning: Maintenance failure; potential visitor casualties and financial loss

safety_signals.visual_form: Rides operating smoothly with intact mechanical animations
safety_signals.meaning: Maintenance staff performing duties; attractions safe and profitable

safety_signals.visual_form: Visitor sprites animated with upright posture; high foot traffic
safety_signals.meaning: Park conditions satisfactory; visitors spending money, retention high

safety_signals.visual_form: Entertainers visible in park; security guards stationed; mechanics near rides
safety_signals.meaning: Adequate staffing; vandalism prevented, facilities maintained, entertainment active

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear walkways with landscaping (trees, fountains) visible
safety_signals.meaning: Handymen performing cleanup duties; park aesthetics high, visitor satisfaction enabled

safety_signals.visual_form: Bus animation at park entrance; visitor count increasing
safety_signals.meaning: Entry pricing and park reputation attract new customers; revenue flowing

safety_signals.visual_form: Research UI elements showing progress; new ride/facility icons unlocking
safety_signals.meaning: Park advancing technologically; future attractions and efficiencies becoming available

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rival parks (competing theme parks in same location/year that affect player performance rankings)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-controlled rides, shops, facilities with consistent placement; bus arrival point; staff uniforms; park signage

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Thugs (hostile variant of visitor entities that vandalize and steal)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Visitor sprite presence throughout park; congregations at rides and shops; happy/sad animation states

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct thug sprite/character model differentiated from regular visitors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear in areas with poor security coverage; cluster near entertainers and food shops; associated with balloon-popping and food-theft animations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Striking staff (absent from posts when negotiation fails)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Entertainers in costumes at show locations; security guards patrolling pathways; mechanics near rides; handymen with broom/cleanup tools

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Early park development (low budget, few facilities)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sparse park layout with minimal rides, single entrance, basic shops, few decorative elements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Low visitor count; staff engaged in basic maintenance and operation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Densely populated park with multiple roller coasters, diverse shops, landscaping features, multiple bus routes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High visitor traffic; crowds at popular attractions; staff efficiently distributed across maintenance and entertainment roles

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visibly deteriorated rides; litter on pathways; absent staff at stations; thugs visible in park spaces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Visitors moving slowly/sad animations; low visitor arrival rate; vandalism animations (balloon popping, theft, violence)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Desert parks show sand/arid terrain; glacier parks show ice/snow environments; different landscape graphics by location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Visitor behavior patterns vary by climate; economic factors (land value, pricing) shift based on regional setting

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Missing staff sprites at their assigned stations; grayed-out shop inventory icons; ride maintenance animations cease
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rides begin deteriorating; pathways accumulate litter; theft/vandalism increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: New ride icons appear in construction menu; facility upgrade animations; staff efficiency visual improvements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Larger bus capacity visually evident; more durable rides show fewer deterioration states; new entertainment acts appear at show venues

world_state_to_visual.world_state: End of fiscal year (performance evaluation period)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: UI displays current park value, rival park rankings, trophies/awards earned
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Visitor influx may increase if park reputation high; rival parks visually demonstrate growth/decline

environmental_storytelling.example: Exploding rides communicate both immediate financial loss and poor management; the visual catastrophe teaches consequence of neglect without explicit UI warning

environmental_storytelling.technique: Visitor density as park health metric
environmental_storytelling.example: High foot traffic and long queues at attractions visually communicate successful design; sparse pathways indicate failed operations

environmental_storytelling.technique: Crime visibility as social health indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Thug sprites appearing in poorly-staffed areas tell story of declining park safety without dialogue; player reads narrative through spatial presence
Desert and glacier terrain variations communicate world geography and economic/climate factors affecting park operations without exposition
Distinct character sprites (entertainer costumes, security uniform, mechanic tools) communicate staff function and social hierarchy within park ecosystem
Gradual visual deterioration of rides (from pristine to damaged animation states to explosion) tells story of time passing and maintenance importance

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Upright posture animation; lingering at attractions; buying merchandise; returning regularly; contributes to high park value
Slumped or animated complaint state; leaving park early; not purchasing items; reduced bus arrivals; triggers thug infiltration
Aggressive movement sprites; targeting entertainers (beating animations); stealing food from shops; popping balloons; causing visible property damage
Stationary at show location; animation cycling (costume-specific); drawing visitor crowds; generates happiness value
Combat animation; knocked down; stops performing; disappears from location temporarily
Positioned at ride; performing repair animations; prevents deterioration state from progressing
Absent from ride; deterioration animations accelerate; ride reaches broken state; explosion occurs
Walking park pathways; interacting with thugs (chasing animations); maintaining crime-free zones
Absent from patrol areas; thugs appear and operate unchecked; vandalism spreads
Positioned on pathways; animation cycle of sweeping/picking up litter; maintains aesthetic cleanliness
Missing from pathways; litter accumulates visually; park aesthetics decline; visitor happiness reduces
Entry animation; visitor sprites disembark; contributes to visitor count and park revenue
Larger bus sprite; more visitor sprites exit; increased capacity efficiency

notes: Theme Park (1994) communicates world state primarily through visual presence/absence of entities and environmental condition states. The game lacks explicit text-based danger warnings; instead, physical manifestations (explosions, thugs, litter, empty staff stations) convey consequences. Visitor happiness drives secondary threat emergence (thug appearance), creating a chain-reaction visual narrative. The simulation paradigm allows player interpretation of visual signals to guide decision-making without overt UI instruction. Geographic location variables (desert, glacier, UK terrain) are the primary world-state differentiators affecting both visual presentation and economic simulation parameters. Crime/safety is communicated entirely through thug sprite presence and interaction animations, making spatial security coverage a visible resource-allocation challenge.
